operating_state: Caterpillar achieved 2024 sales and revenues of $64.8 billion, declining 3% from prior year driven by lower equipment sales volumes across Construction Industries and Resource Industries, partially offset by Energy & Transportation growth led by Power Generation expansion from data center demand. Operating profit margin improved to 20.2% from 19.3% despite volume headwinds, with favorable price realization and manufacturing cost improvements offsetting lower sales volume impact. Order backlog increased to $30.0 billion from $27.5 billion.
cost_structure: Cost of goods sold decreased to $40.2 billion from $42.8 billion, improving gross margin performance through disciplined pricing and lower freight costs despite inflationary pressures on materials and labor. Operating expenses increased with strategic investments in services growth, autonomy, and electrification initiatives. The company expects $250-350 million in tariff-related costs for Q2 2025, with Construction Industries bearing half the impact.
material_events: The company executed aggressive capital returns with $7.7 billion in share repurchases during 2024 compared to $5.0 billion in 2023, reducing outstanding shares to 477.9 million, while maintaining quarterly dividends at $1.41 per share. Board approved additional $20.0 billion share repurchase authorization in June 2024 with $20.1 billion remaining capacity. Restructuring activities related to divestitures generated full-year costs of approximately $400 million.
macro_exposures: The company faces significant tariff exposure with announced 2025 tariffs expected to generate $250-350 million in Q2 costs before mitigation efforts, while foreign currency translation created $556 million in losses reflecting dollar strength. China excavator demand above 10-tons remains at relatively low levels amid property sector challenges, while weak European economic conditions are anticipated to continue into 2025.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2025 sales to be slightly lower than 2024 due to lower sales volume and unfavorable price realization, though services revenues are expected to grow across all segments supported by record backlog strength. Capital expenditures are projected at approximately $2.5 billion in 2025, with restructuring costs and annual effective tax rate of 23.0% excluding discrete items. The company maintains focus on returning substantially all ME&T free cash flow to shareholders while preserving mid-A credit rating.
uncertainty_profile: Tariff impact calculations remain uncertain given unknown final rates and timing of mitigation actions, preventing accurate full-year 2025 net impact estimates beyond Q2 projections. The company maintains unrecognized tax benefits with ongoing examinations creating potential for additional assessments. Dealer inventory management remains subject to independent dealer expectations and macroeconomic conditions, while customer capital discipline in Resource Industries continues despite high utilization rates and elevated fleet age.
